---

### T016: Content Script Injection & Selector Module

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 2 (AI response extraction — selector isolation)
**Depends on:** T011, T012
**Blocks:** T017
**User Stories:** US-02
**Estimated scope:** 45 min

#### Description

Create the content script that injects on claude.ai pages. Build an isolated selector module that encapsulates all DOM selectors for Claude.ai's conversation UI, making them easy to update when Claude.ai changes its DOM structure.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Content script runs on `claude.ai` pages and logs injection confirmation
- [ ] Selector module exports functions: `getConversationContainer()`, `getAssistantMessages()`, `getMessageText(element)`, `getMessageId(element)`
- [ ] Selectors use resilient strategies (data-* attributes, ARIA roles, class patterns)
- [ ] Selector module is a single file that can be updated without changing capture logic
- [ ] Failing selectors return null/empty rather than throwing

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/content/index.ts` — (modify) add injection logic and import selectors
- `packages/extension/src/content/selectors.ts` — (create) isolated Claude.ai DOM selector module
- `packages/extension/src/content/types.ts` — (create) content-script-specific types if needed

#### Implementation Notes

Selectors for Claude.ai are fragile. The key strategy is isolating them in one file. Use a cascade approach: try `[data-testid]` attributes first, fall back to ARIA roles (`[role="presentation"]`, `[role="row"]`), then class-based selectors as last resort. `getConversationContainer()` should find the main conversation scroll area. `getAssistantMessages()` returns all assistant-turn elements. Export a `SELECTORS` object with the raw selector strings for easy updating. The rest of the capture logic (T017) should only call these functions, never use selectors directly.

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Content script loads on a mock claude.ai page without errors
- [ ] Selector module exports are importable and typed

---

### T017: MutationObserver & Streaming Debounce

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 2 (MutationObserver, streaming debounce, AI pool storage)
**Depends on:** T016, T012
**Blocks:** T018, T022
**User Stories:** US-02
**Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours

#### Description

Implement the MutationObserver that watches for new assistant messages in Claude.ai's conversation container. Add streaming debounce logic that waits for a message to stop growing before capturing it. This is the core AI response capture mechanism.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] MutationObserver attaches to the conversation container found by the selector module
- [ ] New assistant message elements trigger the capture pipeline
- [ ] Streaming debounce: capture waits until message text stops changing for 2 seconds
- [ ] Only assistant messages are captured (user messages filtered out)
- [ ] Observer reconnects if the conversation container is replaced (SPA navigation)

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/content/observer.ts` — (create) MutationObserver setup and message detection
- `packages/extension/src/content/debounce.ts` — (create) streaming debounce utility
- `packages/extension/src/content/index.ts` — (modify) wire up observer on page load

#### Implementation Notes

The MutationObserver watches for `childList` changes on the conversation container. When a new child matching the assistant message selector appears, start tracking it. For streaming debounce: set a 2-second timeout that resets every time the message element's `textContent.length` changes. Only when the timeout fires (message stopped growing) do we capture. Use `requestAnimationFrame` or `setTimeout` for the debounce. Handle SPA navigation: Claude.ai is a SPA, so the conversation container may be replaced. Watch for the container disappearing and re-query. Consider using a `MutationObserver` on `document.body` as a top-level watcher that re-attaches the conversation observer when needed.

```typescript
// Streaming debounce pattern
let debounceTimer: number | null = null;
let lastLength = 0;

function onMessageMutation(messageEl: Element) {
  const currentLength = messageEl.textContent?.length ?? 0;
  if (currentLength !== lastLength) {
    lastLength = currentLength;
    if (debounceTimer) clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    debounceTimer = window.setTimeout(() => captureMessage(messageEl), 2000);
  }
}
```

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Unit test: mock DOM mutations simulate streaming, verify capture fires after 2s inactivity
- [ ] Unit test: user messages are not captured
- [ ] Unit test: observer reconnects after container replacement

---

### T018: AI Knowledge Pool Storage

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 2 (Store in chrome.storage.local as AI knowledge pool entries)
**Depends on:** T017, T012
**Blocks:** T022
**User Stories:** US-02
**Estimated scope:** 45 min

#### Description

Implement the storage layer for captured AI responses. Write captured messages to `chrome.storage.local` as AI knowledge pool entries with unique IDs, full text, timestamps, and conversation context.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Captured AI responses are stored in chrome.storage.local under the `ai_pool` key
- [ ] Each entry has: id (unique), text (full response), timestamp, conversationId
- [ ] Duplicate detection: same message ID is not stored twice
- [ ] Storage uses `unlimitedStorage` permission (no quota issues)
- [ ] Storage read/write functions are exported for use by other extension components

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/storage/ai-pool.ts` — (create) AI pool CRUD operations on chrome.storage
- `packages/extension/src/storage/index.ts` — (create) barrel export for storage utilities
- `packages/extension/src/content/observer.ts` — (modify) call storage write after capture

#### Implementation Notes

Use `chrome.storage.local.get('ai_pool')` / `chrome.storage.local.set({ ai_pool: [...] })`. Store as an array of `AIPoolEntry` objects (from `@manum/shared`). Generate unique IDs with `crypto.randomUUID()`. For duplicate detection, check `entry.id` (message ID from DOM) before inserting. Wrap chrome.storage calls in async helper functions:

```typescript
export async function addAIPoolEntry(entry: AIPoolEntry): Promise<void> {
  const { ai_pool = [] } = await chrome.storage.local.get('ai_pool');
  if (ai_pool.some((e: AIPoolEntry) => e.id === entry.id)) return;
  ai_pool.push(entry);
  await chrome.storage.local.set({ ai_pool });
}
```

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Unit test: adding an entry stores it correctly
- [ ] Unit test: duplicate entries are rejected
- [ ] Unit test: entries have all required fields

---

### T019: Copy Event Listener & Record Storage

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 3 (Copy event listener)
**Depends on:** T011, T012
**Blocks:** T022
**User Stories:** US-03
**Estimated scope:** 45 min

#### Description

Add a copy event listener to the content script that captures text selections on Claude.ai pages. Store copy records with the selected text, source AI message ID, and timestamp.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Copy events on claude.ai pages are intercepted
- [ ] Copy record captures: selected text, source message ID, timestamp
- [ ] Source message ID is determined by finding which assistant message contains the selection
- [ ] Multiple copies from the same message create separate records
- [ ] Copy records are stored in chrome.storage.local under the `copy_records` key

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/content/copy-capture.ts` — (create) copy event handler
- `packages/extension/src/storage/copy-records.ts` — (create) copy record CRUD operations
- `packages/extension/src/content/index.ts` — (modify) attach copy event listener

#### Implementation Notes

Listen for the `'copy'` event on `document`. On copy, get the selected text via `window.getSelection().toString()`. To find the source message ID, walk up the DOM from the selection's anchorNode to find the nearest assistant message element, then extract its ID using the selector module. Store in chrome.storage.local:

```typescript
document.addEventListener('copy', async () => {
  const selectedText = window.getSelection()?.toString();
  if (!selectedText?.trim()) return;
  const anchorNode = window.getSelection()?.anchorNode;
  const messageEl = findParentMessage(anchorNode);
  const messageId = messageEl ? getMessageId(messageEl) : undefined;
  await addCopyRecord({
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    selectedText,
    sourceMessageId: messageId,
    timestamp: Date.now(),
  });
});
```

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Unit test: copy event with selection creates a record with correct fields
- [ ] Unit test: copy without selection does not create a record
- [ ] Unit test: source message ID is correctly identified

---

### T020: Tab Focus Tracking

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 4 (Tab tracking)
**Depends on:** T011, T012
**Blocks:** T022
**User Stories:** US-04
**Estimated scope:** 45 min

#### Description

Implement tab focus tracking in both the service worker (chrome.tabs.onActivated) and content scripts (document.visibilitychange). Log timestamped tab-switch events categorized as editor, Claude.ai, or other.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `chrome.tabs.onActivated` listener in service worker logs tab switches
- [ ] `document.visibilitychange` listener in content script logs focus/blur events
- [ ] Each event includes: timestamp, tabType (editor | claude | other), eventType (activated | visible | hidden)
- [ ] Tab events are stored in chrome.storage.local under the `tab_events` key
- [ ] Editor tab is identified by its `chrome-extension://` URL pattern

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/background/tab-tracker.ts` — (create) service worker tab tracking
- `packages/extension/src/content/visibility.ts` — (create) visibility change handler for content script
- `packages/extension/src/storage/tab-events.ts` — (create) tab event storage
- `packages/extension/src/background/service-worker.ts` — (modify) import and init tab tracker
- `packages/extension/src/content/index.ts` — (modify) import and init visibility handler

#### Implementation Notes

In the service worker, use `chrome.tabs.onActivated.addListener` to detect tab switches. Query the tab URL via `chrome.tabs.get(tabId)` to classify: `claude.ai` → 'claude', `chrome-extension://` → 'editor', everything else → 'other'. In the content script, use `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` and check `document.visibilityState`. Both write to `tab_events` in chrome.storage.local. Keep the event log bounded (e.g., last 10,000 events) with oldest-first eviction.

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Unit test: tab activation events are logged with correct tabType
- [ ] Unit test: visibility change events are logged
- [ ] Unit test: editor tab is correctly identified by URL pattern

---

### T021: Offscreen Document & Clipboard Access

**PRD Reference:** Sprint 1, Task 5 (Offscreen document for clipboard)
**Depends on:** T011
**Blocks:** T022
**User Stories:** US-05
**Estimated scope:** 45 min

#### Description

Implement the Manifest V3 offscreen document for clipboard access. Set up message passing between the service worker and the offscreen document. The offscreen document reads clipboard text via `navigator.clipboard.readText()` as a fallback for unlinked paste detection.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Offscreen document is created via `chrome.offscreen.createDocument()` when needed
- [ ] Message passing works: service worker sends request, offscreen document returns clipboard text
- [ ] `navigator.clipboard.readText()` is called in the offscreen document context
- [ ] Offscreen document is created on-demand and handles the case where it already exists
- [ ] Error handling for clipboard permission denied or unavailable

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/offscreen/offscreen.ts` — (modify) implement clipboard read and message listener
- `packages/extension/src/offscreen/offscreen.html` — (modify) ensure script is loaded
- `packages/extension/src/background/clipboard.ts` — (create) service worker side: create offscreen doc, send/receive messages
- `packages/extension/src/background/service-worker.ts` — (modify) import clipboard module

#### Implementation Notes

Creating the offscreen document:
```typescript
async function ensureOffscreenDocument() {
  const existingContexts = await chrome.runtime.getContexts({
    contextTypes: [chrome.runtime.ContextType.OFFSCREEN_DOCUMENT],
  });
  if (existingContexts.length > 0) return;
  await chrome.offscreen.createDocument({
    url: 'src/offscreen/offscreen.html',
    reasons: [chrome.offscreen.Reason.CLIPBOARD],
    justification: 'Read clipboard for unlinked paste detection',
  });
}
```

In the offscreen document, listen for messages:
```typescript
chrome.runtime.onMessage.addListener((msg, sender, sendResponse) => {
  if (msg.type === 'read-clipboard') {
    navigator.clipboard.readText()
      .then(text => sendResponse({ text }))
      .catch(err => sendResponse({ error: err.message }));
    return true; // async response
  }
});
```

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] Unit test: offscreen document creation succeeds
- [ ] Unit test: message passing round-trip returns clipboard text
- [ ] Unit test: error handling when clipboard is unavailable

---

### T022: Capture Layer Unit Tests

**PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Unit tests for Sprint 1)
**Depends on:** T017, T018, T019, T020, T021, T014
**Blocks:** T023
**User Stories:** N/A (infrastructure)

Write comprehensive unit tests for all capture layer components: DOM selectors, MutationObserver, streaming debounce, copy event handler, AI pool storage, tab tracking, and offscreen clipboard.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Storage tests: verify AI pool entries have required fields, duplicates rejected
- [ ] Tab event tests: mock tab activation, verify timestamps and tab types
- [ ] All tests pass: `pnpm --filter @manum/extension test`

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/selectors.test.ts` — (create) DOM selector tests
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/observer.test.ts` — (create) MutationObserver + debounce tests
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/copy-capture.test.ts` — (create) copy event tests
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/ai-pool.test.ts` — (create) AI pool storage tests
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/tab-tracker.test.ts` — (create) tab tracking tests
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/clipboard.test.ts` — (create) offscreen clipboard tests

#### Implementation Notes

Use the chrome API mocks from T014. For DOM tests, create mock DOM structures matching Claude.ai's layout (create elements with the expected selectors/attributes). For the MutationObserver test, use `jest.useFakeTimers()` to control debounce timing. For storage tests, mock `chrome.storage.local` with an in-memory object. Test edge cases: empty selections, missing message IDs, storage quota errors, observer on empty page.

#### Evaluation Checklist

- [ ] At least 15 test cases covering all capture components

### T023: Capture Layer Integration Tests

**PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Integration tests for Sprint 1)
**Depends on:** T022
**Blocks:** None

#### Description

Write integration tests that verify cross-component flows: content script → storage round-trip, message passing chains, and observer → storage pipeline.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test: copy event in content script creates a record readable from service worker context
- [ ] Test: offscreen document message passing chain works end-to-end (mock)

- [ ] All integration tests pass

#### Files to Create/Modify

- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/integration/capture-pipeline.test.ts` — (create) observer → storage flow
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/integration/message-passing.test.ts` — (create) cross-context messaging
- `packages/extension/src/__tests__/integration/storage-roundtrip.test.ts` — (create) write → read verification

#### Implementation Notes

Integration tests use the same chrome API mocks but test multiple modules together. For the capture pipeline test: set up mock DOM → trigger mutations → verify storage contains expected entries. For message passing: mock `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` and `chrome.runtime.onMessage` to simulate cross-context communication. These tests run in Jest (Node environment) with mocked browser APIs.

# Phase 2: Editor Core, Aesthetic & Persistence ## Prerequisites - Phase 1 complete: monorepo builds, extension loads in Chrome, capture layer stores data - `pnpm install` succeeds, `pnpm lint` clean, all Phase 1 tests pass - `@manum/shared` types package exports `AttributionSpan`, `AIPoolEntry`, `CopyRecord`, etc. ## Phase Goal A TipTap-based rich text editor with manuscript aesthetic, running as a chrome-extension:// page, with IndexedDB persistence (auto-save, document list), and real-time data sync from the Chrome extension's capture layer. ## Phase Evaluation Criteria - `pnpm --filter @manum/editor build` produces a working chrome-extension page - Editor loads in a browser and accepts text input with basic formatting (bold, italic, headings) - Manuscript aesthetic is visible: Special Elite font, parchment background, wired-elements controls, rough.js borders - Paper texture overlay renders at ~5% opacity - Documents auto-save to IndexedDB after 2 seconds of inactivity - Document list view displays saved documents with titles and last-modified dates - AI pool entries written to chrome.storage by the extension appear in the editor's IndexedDB within 2 seconds - Connection status indicator shows connected/disconnected state - Editor functions without errors when chrome.storage APIs are unavailable - `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` passes all unit and integration tests - All quality gates green (lint, types, tests) --- ## Tasks ### T030: TipTap Editor Setup **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (React + TipTap editor setup) **Depends on:** T013 **Blocks:** T031, T032, T033 **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Set up the TipTap editor inside the React app. Configure the editor with StarterKit extensions, render it in a centered 768px column layout, and ensure it runs as a chrome-extension:// page. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TipTap editor renders in a React component with editable content area - [ ] Editor is centered with 768px max-width column - [ ] StarterKit extensions loaded (paragraph, heading, bold, italic, etc.) - [ ] Editor accepts keyboard input and produces structured JSON document model - [ ] Page works when loaded as a chrome-extension:// URL #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) replace placeholder with editor layout - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (create) TipTap editor component - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.css` — (create) editor layout styles (centered column) - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add TipTap dependencies #### Implementation Notes Install: `@tiptap/react`, `@tiptap/starter-kit`, `@tiptap/pm`. Use `useEditor` hook from `@tiptap/react`: ```tsx import { useEditor, EditorContent } from '@tiptap/react'; import StarterKit from '@tiptap/starter-kit'; function Editor() { const editor = useEditor({ extensions: [StarterKit], content: '

Start writing...

', }); return (
); } ``` CSS: `.editor-container { max-width: 768px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem; min-height: 100vh; }`. For chrome-extension:// page, the editor's `index.html` will be referenced in the extension manifest later. During development, use `pnpm dev` to test locally. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Editor renders and accepts text input - [ ] Document model is accessible via `editor.getJSON()` --- ### T031: Custom TipTap Marks & Nodes **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (Custom marks for attribution spans, custom nodes for branch markers) **Depends on:** T030, T012 **Blocks:** T060, T066 **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Create custom TipTap marks for attribution spans (storing color, confidence, source metadata) and custom nodes for branch markers. These are the foundational editor primitives that all attribution and branching features build on. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `AttributionMark` custom mark stores: color, confidence, scoring_mode, paste_event_id, original_paste_content, edit_distance_from_paste, created_at - [ ] `BranchMarker` custom node marks section boundaries with branch metadata - [ ] Marks serialize to/from JSON correctly (TipTap document model round-trip) - [ ] Multiple overlapping attribution marks on the same text range work correctly - [ ] Marks are invisible by default (no visual rendering until overlay is toggled) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/extensions/attribution-mark.ts` — (create) custom TipTap mark for attribution - `packages/editor/src/extensions/branch-marker.ts` — (create) custom TipTap node for branch markers - `packages/editor/src/extensions/index.ts` — (create) barrel export for custom extensions - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) register custom extensions #### Implementation Notes TipTap custom mark: ```typescript import { Mark, mergeAttributes } from '@tiptap/core'; export const AttributionMark = Mark.create({ name: 'attribution', addAttributes() { return { color: { default: 'green' }, confidence: { default: 1.0 }, scoring_mode: { default: 'edit-distance' }, paste_event_id: { default: null }, original_paste_content: { default: null }, edit_distance_from_paste: { default: null }, created_at: { default: null }, }; }, parseHTML() { return [{ tag: 'span[data-attribution]' }]; }, renderHTML({ HTMLAttributes }) { return ['span', mergeAttributes({ 'data-attribution': '' }, HTMLAttributes), 0]; }, }); ``` The `BranchMarker` is a block-level node (not inline mark) that wraps paragraphs to indicate they belong to a branched section. It stores `branchId` and visual state. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: attribution mark added to text range, JSON export includes all attributes - [ ] Unit test: mark round-trips through JSON serialize/deserialize --- ### T032: Formatting Toolbar **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (Basic toolbar: bold, italic, headings) **Depends on:** T030 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 30 min #### Description Add a minimal formatting toolbar above the editor with bold, italic, and heading buttons. Toolbar uses wired-elements buttons (or plain buttons initially — aesthetic comes in T034). #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toolbar renders above the editor content area - [ ] Bold button toggles bold on selected text (Ctrl+B also works) - [ ] Italic button toggles italic on selected text (Ctrl+I also works) - [ ] Heading dropdown or buttons for H1, H2, H3 - [ ] Active formatting state is reflected in toolbar button appearance #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` — (create) formatting toolbar component - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.css` — (create) toolbar styles - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) include Toolbar, pass editor instance #### Implementation Notes TipTap provides `editor.chain().focus().toggleBold().run()` for formatting commands. Check active state with `editor.isActive('bold')`. Keep the toolbar minimal — this is a writing tool, not a word processor. The toolbar will be restyled with wired-elements in T034. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Bold, italic, and heading buttons function correctly - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+B, Ctrl+I) work --- ### T033: Font Loading & Color Palette **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction; US-07 **Depends on:** T030 **Blocks:** T034, T035 **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Load the three mandated fonts (Special Elite, Courier Prime, Caveat) and establish the warm parchment color palette as CSS custom properties. Apply body text styling to the editor. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Special Elite font loads and is applied to editor body text - [ ] Courier Prime font loads and is available for metadata/timestamps - [ ] Caveat font loads and is available for handwritten elements - [ ] CSS custom properties defined for the full color palette - [ ] Editor background uses warm parchment tone (#FBF3E3 or #FFF8EF) - [ ] Text color uses dark ink (#080200 or #1E1B12) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/styles/fonts.css` — (create) @font-face or Google Fonts imports - `packages/editor/src/styles/palette.css` — (create) CSS custom properties for colors - `packages/editor/src/styles/global.css` — (create) global body + editor text styles - `packages/editor/src/main.tsx` — (modify) import global styles - `packages/editor/index.html` — (modify) add Google Fonts link if using CDN #### Implementation Notes Use Google Fonts CDN for simplicity: ```html ``` CSS custom properties: ```css :root { --color-parchment: #FBF3E3; --color-parchment-light: #FFF8EF; --color-parchment-dark: #E1D9CA; --color-ink: #080200; --color-ink-soft: #1E1B12; --color-accent: #4A5E8A; --color-warm-gray: #80756D; --color-warm-gray-light: #D2C4BA; --color-gold: #E5C276; --font-body: 'Special Elite', cursive; --font-meta: 'Courier Prime', monospace; --font-hand: 'Caveat', cursive; } ``` Apply to editor: `body { font-family: var(--font-body); background: var(--color-parchment); color: var(--color-ink); }`. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Editor text renders in Special Elite font - [ ] Background is warm parchment color --- ### T034: Rough.js & Wired-Elements Integration **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction (visual library stack); US-07 **Depends on:** T033 **Blocks:** T035 **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Integrate rough.js for hand-drawn borders/shapes and wired-elements for sketchy UI controls. Replace standard HTML buttons in the toolbar with wired-elements components. Create reusable React wrappers for wired-elements. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] wired-elements components render in React (wired-button, wired-toggle, wired-input) - [ ] Toolbar buttons use wired-button or wired-icon-button for sketchy appearance - [ ] rough.js is available for drawing hand-drawn borders and shapes - [ ] React wrapper components handle wired-elements event binding - [ ] Components render without console errors #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredButton.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-button - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredToggle.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-toggle - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredInput.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-input - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/RoughBorder.tsx` — (create) rough.js border component - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/index.ts` — (create) barrel export - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` — (modify) use wired-button components - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add rough.js, wired-elements dependencies #### Implementation Notes wired-elements are web components. In React, they need ref-based event handling since React's synthetic events don't work with custom elements. Create thin wrappers: ```tsx import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'; import 'wired-elements/lib/wired-button'; export function WiredButton({ onClick, children, ...props }) { const ref = useRef(null); useEffect(() => { const el = ref.current; if (el && onClick) { el.addEventListener('click', onClick); return () => el.removeEventListener('click', onClick); } }, [onClick]); return {children}; } ``` For rough.js, create a `` component that draws a hand-drawn rectangle around its children using a canvas or SVG overlay. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Toolbar renders with wired-button styling - [ ] rough.js border renders around at least one element --- ### T035: Paper Textures & Card Styling **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction (paper grain, card rotations, css-doodle + feTurbulence) **Depends on:** T033, T034 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Add paper texture overlays using css-doodle + feTurbulence SVG filters at 5% opacity. Apply slight card rotations (-0.5deg to 1.5deg) to panels for a handcrafted feel. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Paper grain texture renders as a subtle overlay across the editor background - [ ] Texture uses SVG feTurbulence filter (no image assets) - [ ] Overlay is at approximately 5% opacity - [ ] Cards/panels have slight random rotations (-0.5deg to 1.5deg) - [ ] Textures do not significantly impact editor scroll performance #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/PaperTexture.tsx` — (create) SVG filter + css-doodle texture component - `packages/editor/src/styles/cards.css` — (create) card rotation and shadow styles - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) add PaperTexture as background layer - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add css-doodle dependency if used #### Implementation Notes SVG feTurbulence approach: ```tsx function PaperTexture() { return ( ); } ``` CSS for the texture layer: `position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0;`. Card rotations: use CSS `transform: rotate(calc(-0.5deg + var(--card-rotation)))` with a CSS custom property set per card (random between -0.5 and 1.5). #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Paper grain texture visible on editor background at subtle opacity - [ ] Card/panel elements show slight rotation --- ### T036: IndexedDB Storage Layer **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Persistence layer — IndexedDB) **Depends on:** T012 **Blocks:** T037, T038, T039 **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Create the IndexedDB storage layer for the editor. Define object stores for documents, attribution spans, and the local copy of the AI knowledge pool. Implement CRUD operations. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] IndexedDB database `manum-editor` with object stores: `documents`, `attributionSpans`, `aiPool`, `copyRecords` - [ ] Document schema: id, title, content (TipTap JSON), createdAt, updatedAt - [ ] CRUD operations: createDocument, getDocument, updateDocument, deleteDocument, listDocuments - [ ] AI pool and copy record stores for synced extension data - [ ] Database versioning with upgrade handler for schema changes - [ ] All operations are async and return typed results #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/storage/db.ts` — (create) IndexedDB initialization and upgrade logic - `packages/editor/src/storage/documents.ts` — (create) document CRUD operations - `packages/editor/src/storage/ai-pool.ts` — (create) AI pool operations (editor-side) - `packages/editor/src/storage/copy-records.ts` — (create) copy record operations (editor-side) - `packages/editor/src/storage/index.ts` — (create) barrel export - `packages/editor/src/storage/types.ts` — (create) storage-specific types (DocumentRecord, etc.) #### Implementation Notes Use the raw IndexedDB API (no library) to keep bundle size minimal. Wrap in a helper class or functions: ```typescript const DB_NAME = 'manum-editor'; const DB_VERSION = 1; function openDB(): Promise { return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION); request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => { const db = request.result; if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains('documents')) { const store = db.createObjectStore('documents', { keyPath: 'id' }); store.createIndex('updatedAt', 'updatedAt'); } // ... other stores }; request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result); request.onerror = () => reject(request.error); }); } ``` Document content is stored as the TipTap JSON document model (`editor.getJSON()`). Keep a `title` field derived from the first heading or first line of text. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: create, read, update, delete document operations work - [ ] Unit test: listDocuments returns all documents sorted by updatedAt --- ### T037: Auto-Save with Debounce **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Auto-save, debounced 2 seconds of inactivity) **Depends on:** T036, T030 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Implement auto-save that triggers after 2 seconds of edit inactivity. Connect TipTap's `onUpdate` callback to the IndexedDB storage layer with debounce. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Editor content auto-saves to IndexedDB after 2 seconds of no edits - [ ] Rapid edits reset the debounce timer (only one save after activity stops) - [ ] Save includes the full TipTap JSON document model and updated timestamp - [ ] A visual indicator (subtle) shows save status (saving... / saved) - [ ] Auto-save does not block the editor UI #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts` — (create) auto-save hook with debounce - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) wire up useAutoSave hook - `packages/editor/src/components/SaveIndicator.tsx` — (create) small save status indicator #### Implementation Notes ```typescript function useAutoSave(editor: Editor | null, documentId: string) { const timeoutRef = useRef(null); const [saveStatus, setSaveStatus] = useState<'saved' | 'saving' | 'unsaved'>('saved'); useEffect(() => { if (!editor) return; const handler = () => { setSaveStatus('unsaved'); if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current); timeoutRef.current = window.setTimeout(async () => { setSaveStatus('saving'); await updateDocument(documentId, { content: editor.getJSON(), updatedAt: Date.now() }); setSaveStatus('saved'); }, 2000); }; editor.on('update', handler); return () => editor.off('update', handler); }, [editor, documentId]); return saveStatus; } ``` The save indicator should be very subtle — e.g., a small dot or text in the corner. Use Caveat font for the "Saved" text. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: debounce fires once after rapid edits stop for 2s - [ ] Unit test: document content in IndexedDB matches editor state after save --- ### T038: Document List View **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Document list view) **Depends on:** T036 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Create a document list view that displays all saved documents with titles and last-modified dates. Support creating new documents, opening existing ones, and deleting documents. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Document list view shows all documents from IndexedDB - [ ] Each entry shows: title (derived from content), last modified date - [ ] Click a document to open it in the editor - [ ] "New Document" button creates a blank document and opens it - [ ] Delete action removes a document from IndexedDB (with confirmation) - [ ] List updates when documents are created/modified/deleted #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/DocumentList.tsx` — (create) document list component - `packages/editor/src/components/DocumentList.css` — (create) list styling (manuscript aesthetic) - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) add routing between document list and editor views - `packages/editor/src/hooks/useDocuments.ts` — (create) hook for document list state #### Implementation Notes Simple client-side routing: use React state (no need for react-router). `App.tsx` holds `currentView: 'list' | 'editor'` and `currentDocumentId`. The list view uses `listDocuments()` from the storage layer. Title derivation: extract text from the first heading node in the TipTap JSON, or first 50 chars of first paragraph. Style with manuscript aesthetic: wired-card for each document entry, Caveat font for dates, rough.js borders. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Document list renders with existing documents - [ ] New document creation works and opens editor - [ ] Deleting a document removes it from the list --- ### T039: Extension-Editor Data Sync **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 7 (Extension <-> Web App communication) **Depends on:** T036, T012 **Blocks:** T040 **User Stories:** US-09 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Sync the AI knowledge pool and copy records from chrome.storage (written by the extension's capture layer) to the editor's IndexedDB. Use `chrome.storage.onChanged` for real-time sync since the editor runs as a chrome-extension:// page with direct API access. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] AI pool entries from chrome.storage sync to editor's IndexedDB `aiPool` store - [ ] Copy records from chrome.storage sync to editor's IndexedDB `copyRecords` store - [ ] `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener triggers sync on new data - [ ] Initial sync on editor load pulls all existing data - [ ] Sync is idempotent — duplicate entries are not created - [ ] Sync works for incremental updates (only new entries synced) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/sync/extension-sync.ts` — (create) sync logic: chrome.storage → IndexedDB - `packages/editor/src/sync/index.ts` — (create) barrel export and initialization - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) initialize sync on app load #### Implementation Notes Since the editor runs as a chrome-extension:// page, it has direct access to `chrome.storage.local`: ```typescript async function initialSync() { const { ai_pool = [], copy_records = [] } = await chrome.storage.local.get(['ai_pool', 'copy_records']); await syncAIPoolToIDB(ai_pool); await syncCopyRecordsToIDB(copy_records); } chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener((changes, area) => { if (area !== 'local') return; if (changes.ai_pool) syncAIPoolToIDB(changes.ai_pool.newValue ?? []); if (changes.copy_records) syncCopyRecordsToIDB(changes.copy_records.newValue ?? []); }); ``` The sync functions should be idempotent: check for existing entries by ID before inserting. Use a simple "last synced" watermark if needed for performance, but start with full comparison. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: AI pool entries sync from chrome.storage mock to IndexedDB mock - [ ] Unit test: incremental sync handles new entries without duplicating existing ones - [ ] Unit test: chrome.storage.onChanged triggers sync callback --- ### T040: Connection Status & Graceful Degradation **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 7 (Graceful degradation, connection status) **Depends on:** T039 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-09 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Add a connection status indicator showing whether the extension data source is connected. Implement graceful degradation so the editor works without extension APIs — all new text is treated as GREEN. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Connection status indicator visible in the editor UI (connected/disconnected) - [ ] Status checks for chrome.storage API availability - [ ] When disconnected: editor functions normally, paste detection skips AI matching, all text is GREEN - [ ] Previously synced data in IndexedDB remains available even when disconnected - [ ] No console errors when chrome APIs are unavailable #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/sync/connection-status.ts` — (create) connection detection logic - `packages/editor/src/components/ConnectionIndicator.tsx` — (create) UI indicator component - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) include ConnectionIndicator - `packages/editor/src/sync/extension-sync.ts` — (modify) add try/catch for API unavailability #### Implementation Notes Detection: check if `typeof chrome !== 'undefined' && chrome.storage?.local`. If not available, set status to disconnected. The indicator should be small and unobtrusive — a colored dot (green=connected, gray=disconnected) with tooltip. When disconnected, the sync module should no-op gracefully. The editor's attribution logic (Phase 3) will check connection status and skip AI pool matching when disconnected. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Indicator shows "connected" when chrome.storage is available - [ ] Indicator shows "disconnected" when chrome.storage is unavailable - [ ] Editor renders without errors when chrome APIs are absent --- ### T041: Editor Unit Tests **PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Unit tests for Sprint 2) **Depends on:** T030, T031, T036, T037, T038, T039, T040 **Blocks:** T042 **User Stories:** N/A (infrastructure) **Estimated scope:** 2 hours #### Description Write unit tests for all editor core components: TipTap document model, custom marks, IndexedDB operations, auto-save debounce, sync logic, and connection status. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TipTap custom mark tests: attribution mark attributes round-trip through JSON - [ ] IndexedDB tests: CRUD operations work with mock or in-memory DB - [ ] Auto-save tests: debounce fires correctly, rapid edits produce single save - [ ] Sync tests: chrome.storage data syncs to IndexedDB mock - [ ] Connection status tests: detection logic works for available/unavailable APIs - [ ] All tests pass: `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/attribution-mark.test.ts` — (create) custom mark tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/storage.test.ts` — (create) IndexedDB operation tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/auto-save.test.ts` — (create) debounce and save tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/sync.test.ts` — (create) extension sync tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/connection.test.ts` — (create) connection status tests #### Implementation Notes For IndexedDB tests, use `fake-indexeddb` npm package to provide an in-memory IndexedDB implementation in Node.js. For chrome.storage mocks, create a simple mock object. For TipTap tests, create a headless editor instance via `new Editor({ extensions: [...] })` without rendering. Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for debounce tests. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` passes with all tests green - [ ] At least 15 test cases covering all editor core components --- ### T042: Editor Integration Tests **PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Integration tests for Sprint 2) **Depends on:** T041 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** N/A (infrastructure) **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Write integration tests for cross-component flows: editor → auto-save → IndexedDB round-trip, extension sync → IndexedDB → query pipeline, and document list → editor navigation. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Test: edit in TipTap → auto-save → read from IndexedDB → content matches - [ ] Test: chrome.storage sync → IndexedDB → queryable AI pool entries - [ ] Test: create document → appears in list → open → edit → save → reopen → content persists - [ ] All integration tests pass #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/persistence.test.ts` — (create) editor ↔ IndexedDB flow - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/sync-pipeline.test.ts` — (create) sync ↔ query flow - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/document-lifecycle.test.ts` — (create) full document lifecycle #### Implementation Notes Use `fake-indexeddb` and chrome.storage mocks. Create a headless TipTap editor, simulate edits via `editor.commands.insertContent(...)`, trigger auto-save, then verify IndexedDB contents. For the sync pipeline, write to the chrome.storage mock, trigger the onChanged callback, then query the IndexedDB store. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test -- --testPathPattern=integration` passes - [ ] At least 5 integration test cases

# Phase 2: Editor Core, Aesthetic & Persistence ## Prerequisites - Phase 1 complete: monorepo builds, extension loads in Chrome, capture layer stores data - `pnpm install` succeeds, `pnpm lint` clean, all Phase 1 tests pass - `@manum/shared` types package exports `AttributionSpan`, `AIPoolEntry`, `CopyRecord`, etc. ## Phase Goal A TipTap-based rich text editor with manuscript aesthetic, running as a chrome-extension:// page, with IndexedDB persistence (auto-save, document list), and real-time data sync from the Chrome extension's capture layer. ## Phase Evaluation Criteria - `pnpm --filter @manum/editor build` produces a working chrome-extension page - Editor loads in a browser and accepts text input with basic formatting (bold, italic, headings) - Manuscript aesthetic is visible: Special Elite font, parchment background, wired-elements controls, rough.js borders - Paper texture overlay renders at ~5% opacity - Documents auto-save to IndexedDB after 2 seconds of inactivity - Document list view displays saved documents with titles and last-modified dates - AI pool entries written to chrome.storage by the extension appear in the editor's IndexedDB within 2 seconds - Connection status indicator shows connected/disconnected state - Editor functions without errors when chrome.storage APIs are unavailable - `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` passes all unit and integration tests - All quality gates green (lint, types, tests) --- ## Tasks ### T030: TipTap Editor Setup **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (React + TipTap editor setup) **Depends on:** T013 **Blocks:** T031, T032, T033 **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Set up the TipTap editor inside the React app. Configure the editor with StarterKit extensions, render it in a centered 768px column layout, and ensure it runs as a chrome-extension:// page. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TipTap editor renders in a React component with editable content area - [ ] Editor is centered with 768px max-width column - [ ] StarterKit extensions loaded (paragraph, heading, bold, italic, etc.) - [ ] Editor accepts keyboard input and produces structured JSON document model - [ ] Page works when loaded as a chrome-extension:// URL #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) replace placeholder with editor layout - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (create) TipTap editor component - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.css` — (create) editor layout styles (centered column) - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add TipTap dependencies #### Implementation Notes Install: `@tiptap/react`, `@tiptap/starter-kit`, `@tiptap/pm`. Use `useEditor` hook from `@tiptap/react`: ```tsx import { useEditor, EditorContent } from '@tiptap/react'; import StarterKit from '@tiptap/starter-kit'; function Editor() { const editor = useEditor({ extensions: [StarterKit], content: '

Start writing...

', }); return (
); } ``` CSS: `.editor-container { max-width: 768px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 2rem; min-height: 100vh; }`. For chrome-extension:// page, the editor's `index.html` will be referenced in the extension manifest later. During development, use `pnpm dev` to test locally. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Editor renders and accepts text input - [ ] Document model is accessible via `editor.getJSON()` --- ### T031: Custom TipTap Marks & Nodes **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (Custom marks for attribution spans, custom nodes for branch markers) **Depends on:** T030, T012 **Blocks:** T060, T066 **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Create custom TipTap marks for attribution spans (storing color, confidence, source metadata) and custom nodes for branch markers. These are the foundational editor primitives that all attribution and branching features build on. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `AttributionMark` custom mark stores: color, confidence, scoring_mode, paste_event_id, original_paste_content, edit_distance_from_paste, created_at - [ ] `BranchMarker` custom node marks section boundaries with branch metadata - [ ] Marks serialize to/from JSON correctly (TipTap document model round-trip) - [ ] Multiple overlapping attribution marks on the same text range work correctly - [ ] Marks are invisible by default (no visual rendering until overlay is toggled) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/extensions/attribution-mark.ts` — (create) custom TipTap mark for attribution - `packages/editor/src/extensions/branch-marker.ts` — (create) custom TipTap node for branch markers - `packages/editor/src/extensions/index.ts` — (create) barrel export for custom extensions - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) register custom extensions #### Implementation Notes TipTap custom mark: ```typescript import { Mark, mergeAttributes } from '@tiptap/core'; export const AttributionMark = Mark.create({ name: 'attribution', addAttributes() { return { color: { default: 'green' }, confidence: { default: 1.0 }, scoring_mode: { default: 'edit-distance' }, paste_event_id: { default: null }, original_paste_content: { default: null }, edit_distance_from_paste: { default: null }, created_at: { default: null }, }; }, parseHTML() { return [{ tag: 'span[data-attribution]' }]; }, renderHTML({ HTMLAttributes }) { return ['span', mergeAttributes({ 'data-attribution': '' }, HTMLAttributes), 0]; }, }); ``` The `BranchMarker` is a block-level node (not inline mark) that wraps paragraphs to indicate they belong to a branched section. It stores `branchId` and visual state. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: attribution mark added to text range, JSON export includes all attributes - [ ] Unit test: mark round-trips through JSON serialize/deserialize --- ### T032: Formatting Toolbar **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 1 (Basic toolbar: bold, italic, headings) **Depends on:** T030 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-06 **Estimated scope:** 30 min #### Description Add a minimal formatting toolbar above the editor with bold, italic, and heading buttons. Toolbar uses wired-elements buttons (or plain buttons initially — aesthetic comes in T034). #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toolbar renders above the editor content area - [ ] Bold button toggles bold on selected text (Ctrl+B also works) - [ ] Italic button toggles italic on selected text (Ctrl+I also works) - [ ] Heading dropdown or buttons for H1, H2, H3 - [ ] Active formatting state is reflected in toolbar button appearance #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` — (create) formatting toolbar component - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.css` — (create) toolbar styles - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) include Toolbar, pass editor instance #### Implementation Notes TipTap provides `editor.chain().focus().toggleBold().run()` for formatting commands. Check active state with `editor.isActive('bold')`. Keep the toolbar minimal — this is a writing tool, not a word processor. The toolbar will be restyled with wired-elements in T034. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Bold, italic, and heading buttons function correctly - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+B, Ctrl+I) work --- ### T033: Font Loading & Color Palette **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction; US-07 **Depends on:** T030 **Blocks:** T034, T035 **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Load the three mandated fonts (Special Elite, Courier Prime, Caveat) and establish the warm parchment color palette as CSS custom properties. Apply body text styling to the editor. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Special Elite font loads and is applied to editor body text - [ ] Courier Prime font loads and is available for metadata/timestamps - [ ] Caveat font loads and is available for handwritten elements - [ ] CSS custom properties defined for the full color palette - [ ] Editor background uses warm parchment tone (#FBF3E3 or #FFF8EF) - [ ] Text color uses dark ink (#080200 or #1E1B12) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/styles/fonts.css` — (create) @font-face or Google Fonts imports - `packages/editor/src/styles/palette.css` — (create) CSS custom properties for colors - `packages/editor/src/styles/global.css` — (create) global body + editor text styles - `packages/editor/src/main.tsx` — (modify) import global styles - `packages/editor/index.html` — (modify) add Google Fonts link if using CDN #### Implementation Notes Use Google Fonts CDN for simplicity: ```html ``` CSS custom properties: ```css :root { --color-parchment: #FBF3E3; --color-parchment-light: #FFF8EF; --color-parchment-dark: #E1D9CA; --color-ink: #080200; --color-ink-soft: #1E1B12; --color-accent: #4A5E8A; --color-warm-gray: #80756D; --color-warm-gray-light: #D2C4BA; --color-gold: #E5C276; --font-body: 'Special Elite', cursive; --font-meta: 'Courier Prime', monospace; --font-hand: 'Caveat', cursive; } ``` Apply to editor: `body { font-family: var(--font-body); background: var(--color-parchment); color: var(--color-ink); }`. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Editor text renders in Special Elite font - [ ] Background is warm parchment color --- ### T034: Rough.js & Wired-Elements Integration **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction (visual library stack); US-07 **Depends on:** T033 **Blocks:** T035 **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Integrate rough.js for hand-drawn borders/shapes and wired-elements for sketchy UI controls. Replace standard HTML buttons in the toolbar with wired-elements components. Create reusable React wrappers for wired-elements. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] wired-elements components render in React (wired-button, wired-toggle, wired-input) - [ ] Toolbar buttons use wired-button or wired-icon-button for sketchy appearance - [ ] rough.js is available for drawing hand-drawn borders and shapes - [ ] React wrapper components handle wired-elements event binding - [ ] Components render without console errors #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredButton.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-button - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredToggle.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-toggle - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/WiredInput.tsx` — (create) React wrapper for wired-input - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/RoughBorder.tsx` — (create) rough.js border component - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/index.ts` — (create) barrel export - `packages/editor/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` — (modify) use wired-button components - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add rough.js, wired-elements dependencies #### Implementation Notes wired-elements are web components. In React, they need ref-based event handling since React's synthetic events don't work with custom elements. Create thin wrappers: ```tsx import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react'; import 'wired-elements/lib/wired-button'; export function WiredButton({ onClick, children, ...props }) { const ref = useRef(null); useEffect(() => { const el = ref.current; if (el && onClick) { el.addEventListener('click', onClick); return () => el.removeEventListener('click', onClick); } }, [onClick]); return {children}; } ``` For rough.js, create a `` component that draws a hand-drawn rectangle around its children using a canvas or SVG overlay. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Toolbar renders with wired-button styling - [ ] rough.js border renders around at least one element --- ### T035: Paper Textures & Card Styling **PRD Reference:** UI Design Direction (paper grain, card rotations, css-doodle + feTurbulence) **Depends on:** T033, T034 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-07 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Add paper texture overlays using css-doodle + feTurbulence SVG filters at 5% opacity. Apply slight card rotations (-0.5deg to 1.5deg) to panels for a handcrafted feel. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Paper grain texture renders as a subtle overlay across the editor background - [ ] Texture uses SVG feTurbulence filter (no image assets) - [ ] Overlay is at approximately 5% opacity - [ ] Cards/panels have slight random rotations (-0.5deg to 1.5deg) - [ ] Textures do not significantly impact editor scroll performance #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/ui/PaperTexture.tsx` — (create) SVG filter + css-doodle texture component - `packages/editor/src/styles/cards.css` — (create) card rotation and shadow styles - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) add PaperTexture as background layer - `packages/editor/package.json` — (modify) add css-doodle dependency if used #### Implementation Notes SVG feTurbulence approach: ```tsx function PaperTexture() { return ( ); } ``` CSS for the texture layer: `position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0;`. Card rotations: use CSS `transform: rotate(calc(-0.5deg + var(--card-rotation)))` with a CSS custom property set per card (random between -0.5 and 1.5). #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Paper grain texture visible on editor background at subtle opacity - [ ] Card/panel elements show slight rotation --- ### T036: IndexedDB Storage Layer **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Persistence layer — IndexedDB) **Depends on:** T012 **Blocks:** T037, T038, T039 **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Create the IndexedDB storage layer for the editor. Define object stores for documents, attribution spans, and the local copy of the AI knowledge pool. Implement CRUD operations. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] IndexedDB database `manum-editor` with object stores: `documents`, `attributionSpans`, `aiPool`, `copyRecords` - [ ] Document schema: id, title, content (TipTap JSON), createdAt, updatedAt - [ ] CRUD operations: createDocument, getDocument, updateDocument, deleteDocument, listDocuments - [ ] AI pool and copy record stores for synced extension data - [ ] Database versioning with upgrade handler for schema changes - [ ] All operations are async and return typed results #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/storage/db.ts` — (create) IndexedDB initialization and upgrade logic - `packages/editor/src/storage/documents.ts` — (create) document CRUD operations - `packages/editor/src/storage/ai-pool.ts` — (create) AI pool operations (editor-side) - `packages/editor/src/storage/copy-records.ts` — (create) copy record operations (editor-side) - `packages/editor/src/storage/index.ts` — (create) barrel export - `packages/editor/src/storage/types.ts` — (create) storage-specific types (DocumentRecord, etc.) #### Implementation Notes Use the raw IndexedDB API (no library) to keep bundle size minimal. Wrap in a helper class or functions: ```typescript const DB_NAME = 'manum-editor'; const DB_VERSION = 1; function openDB(): Promise { return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION); request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => { const db = request.result; if (!db.objectStoreNames.contains('documents')) { const store = db.createObjectStore('documents', { keyPath: 'id' }); store.createIndex('updatedAt', 'updatedAt'); } // ... other stores }; request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result); request.onerror = () => reject(request.error); }); } ``` Document content is stored as the TipTap JSON document model (`editor.getJSON()`). Keep a `title` field derived from the first heading or first line of text. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: create, read, update, delete document operations work - [ ] Unit test: listDocuments returns all documents sorted by updatedAt --- ### T037: Auto-Save with Debounce **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Auto-save, debounced 2 seconds of inactivity) **Depends on:** T036, T030 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Implement auto-save that triggers after 2 seconds of edit inactivity. Connect TipTap's `onUpdate` callback to the IndexedDB storage layer with debounce. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Editor content auto-saves to IndexedDB after 2 seconds of no edits - [ ] Rapid edits reset the debounce timer (only one save after activity stops) - [ ] Save includes the full TipTap JSON document model and updated timestamp - [ ] A visual indicator (subtle) shows save status (saving... / saved) - [ ] Auto-save does not block the editor UI #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/hooks/useAutoSave.ts` — (create) auto-save hook with debounce - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) wire up useAutoSave hook - `packages/editor/src/components/SaveIndicator.tsx` — (create) small save status indicator #### Implementation Notes ```typescript function useAutoSave(editor: Editor | null, documentId: string) { const timeoutRef = useRef(null); const [saveStatus, setSaveStatus] = useState<'saved' | 'saving' | 'unsaved'>('saved'); useEffect(() => { if (!editor) return; const handler = () => { setSaveStatus('unsaved'); if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current); timeoutRef.current = window.setTimeout(async () => { setSaveStatus('saving'); await updateDocument(documentId, { content: editor.getJSON(), updatedAt: Date.now() }); setSaveStatus('saved'); }, 2000); }; editor.on('update', handler); return () => editor.off('update', handler); }, [editor, documentId]); return saveStatus; } ``` The save indicator should be very subtle — e.g., a small dot or text in the corner. Use Caveat font for the "Saved" text. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: debounce fires once after rapid edits stop for 2s - [ ] Unit test: document content in IndexedDB matches editor state after save --- ### T038: Document List View **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 6 (Document list view) **Depends on:** T036 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-08 **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Create a document list view that displays all saved documents with titles and last-modified dates. Support creating new documents, opening existing ones, and deleting documents. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Document list view shows all documents from IndexedDB - [ ] Each entry shows: title (derived from content), last modified date - [ ] Click a document to open it in the editor - [ ] "New Document" button creates a blank document and opens it - [ ] Delete action removes a document from IndexedDB (with confirmation) - [ ] List updates when documents are created/modified/deleted #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/components/DocumentList.tsx` — (create) document list component - `packages/editor/src/components/DocumentList.css` — (create) list styling (manuscript aesthetic) - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) add routing between document list and editor views - `packages/editor/src/hooks/useDocuments.ts` — (create) hook for document list state #### Implementation Notes Simple client-side routing: use React state (no need for react-router). `App.tsx` holds `currentView: 'list' | 'editor'` and `currentDocumentId`. The list view uses `listDocuments()` from the storage layer. Title derivation: extract text from the first heading node in the TipTap JSON, or first 50 chars of first paragraph. Style with manuscript aesthetic: wired-card for each document entry, Caveat font for dates, rough.js borders. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Document list renders with existing documents - [ ] New document creation works and opens editor - [ ] Deleting a document removes it from the list --- ### T039: Extension-Editor Data Sync **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 7 (Extension <-> Web App communication) **Depends on:** T036, T012 **Blocks:** T040 **User Stories:** US-09 **Estimated scope:** 1.5 hours #### Description Sync the AI knowledge pool and copy records from chrome.storage (written by the extension's capture layer) to the editor's IndexedDB. Use `chrome.storage.onChanged` for real-time sync since the editor runs as a chrome-extension:// page with direct API access. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] AI pool entries from chrome.storage sync to editor's IndexedDB `aiPool` store - [ ] Copy records from chrome.storage sync to editor's IndexedDB `copyRecords` store - [ ] `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener triggers sync on new data - [ ] Initial sync on editor load pulls all existing data - [ ] Sync is idempotent — duplicate entries are not created - [ ] Sync works for incremental updates (only new entries synced) #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/sync/extension-sync.ts` — (create) sync logic: chrome.storage → IndexedDB - `packages/editor/src/sync/index.ts` — (create) barrel export and initialization - `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` — (modify) initialize sync on app load #### Implementation Notes Since the editor runs as a chrome-extension:// page, it has direct access to `chrome.storage.local`: ```typescript async function initialSync() { const { ai_pool = [], copy_records = [] } = await chrome.storage.local.get(['ai_pool', 'copy_records']); await syncAIPoolToIDB(ai_pool); await syncCopyRecordsToIDB(copy_records); } chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener((changes, area) => { if (area !== 'local') return; if (changes.ai_pool) syncAIPoolToIDB(changes.ai_pool.newValue ?? []); if (changes.copy_records) syncCopyRecordsToIDB(changes.copy_records.newValue ?? []); }); ``` The sync functions should be idempotent: check for existing entries by ID before inserting. Use a simple "last synced" watermark if needed for performance, but start with full comparison. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Unit test: AI pool entries sync from chrome.storage mock to IndexedDB mock - [ ] Unit test: incremental sync handles new entries without duplicating existing ones - [ ] Unit test: chrome.storage.onChanged triggers sync callback --- ### T040: Connection Status & Graceful Degradation **PRD Reference:** Sprint 2, Task 7 (Graceful degradation, connection status) **Depends on:** T039 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** US-09 **Estimated scope:** 45 min #### Description Add a connection status indicator showing whether the extension data source is connected. Implement graceful degradation so the editor works without extension APIs — all new text is treated as GREEN. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Connection status indicator visible in the editor UI (connected/disconnected) - [ ] Status checks for chrome.storage API availability - [ ] When disconnected: editor functions normally, paste detection skips AI matching, all text is GREEN - [ ] Previously synced data in IndexedDB remains available even when disconnected - [ ] No console errors when chrome APIs are unavailable #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/sync/connection-status.ts` — (create) connection detection logic - `packages/editor/src/components/ConnectionIndicator.tsx` — (create) UI indicator component - `packages/editor/src/components/Editor.tsx` — (modify) include ConnectionIndicator - `packages/editor/src/sync/extension-sync.ts` — (modify) add try/catch for API unavailability #### Implementation Notes Detection: check if `typeof chrome !== 'undefined' && chrome.storage?.local`. If not available, set status to disconnected. The indicator should be small and unobtrusive — a colored dot (green=connected, gray=disconnected) with tooltip. When disconnected, the sync module should no-op gracefully. The editor's attribution logic (Phase 3) will check connection status and skip AI pool matching when disconnected. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] Indicator shows "connected" when chrome.storage is available - [ ] Indicator shows "disconnected" when chrome.storage is unavailable - [ ] Editor renders without errors when chrome APIs are absent --- ### T041: Editor Unit Tests **PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Unit tests for Sprint 2) **Depends on:** T030, T031, T036, T037, T038, T039, T040 **Blocks:** T042 **User Stories:** N/A (infrastructure) **Estimated scope:** 2 hours #### Description Write unit tests for all editor core components: TipTap document model, custom marks, IndexedDB operations, auto-save debounce, sync logic, and connection status. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TipTap custom mark tests: attribution mark attributes round-trip through JSON - [ ] IndexedDB tests: CRUD operations work with mock or in-memory DB - [ ] Auto-save tests: debounce fires correctly, rapid edits produce single save - [ ] Sync tests: chrome.storage data syncs to IndexedDB mock - [ ] Connection status tests: detection logic works for available/unavailable APIs - [ ] All tests pass: `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/attribution-mark.test.ts` — (create) custom mark tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/storage.test.ts` — (create) IndexedDB operation tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/auto-save.test.ts` — (create) debounce and save tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/sync.test.ts` — (create) extension sync tests - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/connection.test.ts` — (create) connection status tests #### Implementation Notes For IndexedDB tests, use `fake-indexeddb` npm package to provide an in-memory IndexedDB implementation in Node.js. For chrome.storage mocks, create a simple mock object. For TipTap tests, create a headless editor instance via `new Editor({ extensions: [...] })` without rendering. Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for debounce tests. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test` passes with all tests green - [ ] At least 15 test cases covering all editor core components --- ### T042: Editor Integration Tests **PRD Reference:** Testing Strategy (Integration tests for Sprint 2) **Depends on:** T041 **Blocks:** None **User Stories:** N/A (infrastructure) **Estimated scope:** 1 hour #### Description Write integration tests for cross-component flows: editor → auto-save → IndexedDB round-trip, extension sync → IndexedDB → query pipeline, and document list → editor navigation. #### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Test: edit in TipTap → auto-save → read from IndexedDB → content matches - [ ] Test: chrome.storage sync → IndexedDB → queryable AI pool entries - [ ] Test: create document → appears in list → open → edit → save → reopen → content persists - [ ] All integration tests pass #### Files to Create/Modify - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/persistence.test.ts` — (create) editor ↔ IndexedDB flow - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/sync-pipeline.test.ts` — (create) sync ↔ query flow - `packages/editor/src/__tests__/integration/document-lifecycle.test.ts` — (create) full document lifecycle #### Implementation Notes Use `fake-indexeddb` and chrome.storage mocks. Create a headless TipTap editor, simulate edits via `editor.commands.insertContent(...)`, trigger auto-save, then verify IndexedDB contents. For the sync pipeline, write to the chrome.storage mock, trigger the onChanged callback, then query the IndexedDB store. #### Evaluation Checklist - [ ] `pnpm --filter @manum/editor test -- --testPathPattern=integration` passes - [ ] At least 5 integration test cases
